enonce.texte: Match each word related to government and democracy (Lesson 18) with its correct definition. Write the letter of the definition next to the number of the word.

questions.texte: Match the words to their definitions.

sousQuestions.reponse: c. A system of government where citizens choose their leaders by voting.
e. A formal event where people vote to choose a person for a political position.
a. A legal member of a country who has rights and responsibilities.
b. A rule made by the government that people must follow.
d. Basic freedoms and protections that every person should have (e.g., freedom of speech).

enonce.texte: Read the descriptions below. Identify which form of government (democracy, monarchy, or dictatorship) is being described. Then, justify your answer with one element from the text.

questions.texte: In Country A, citizens vote every five years to choose their president and members of parliament. The government must respect the constitution and the courts are independent.
questions.reponse: Democracy. Justification: 'citizens vote' and 'courts are independent' show popular participation and rule of law.

questions.texte: In Country B, the king holds all the power. He decides the laws, controls the army, and his son will become the next king. Citizens cannot vote for their leaders.
questions.reponse: Monarchy. Justification: 'the king holds all the power' and 'his son will become the next king' indicate hereditary rule.

questions.texte: In Country C, one man, General Zoda, took power by force. He controls the media, arrests his opponents, and citizens have no right to criticize him. There are no elections.
questions.reponse: Dictatorship. Justification: 'took power by force' and 'citizens have no right to criticize him' show absolute control without citizen participation.

intitule: Applying Democratic Principles in a Cameroonian School

enonce.texte: You are the president of the student council at your secondary school in Yaoundé. The principal wants to create a new rule: 'All students must wear a specific hairstyle.' Many students disagree. Propose a democratic solution to this problem. In your answer, use at least four of the following vocabulary words: democracy, election, vote, citizen, law, representative, human rights, government. Then, explain how your solution respects the principle of democracy.

questions.texte: Write your proposed solution and explanation.
questions.reponse: As a student representative in a democracy, I would propose that instead of imposing a law, the school government should organize an election where every student citizen can vote on the hairstyle rule. We could create two options: 'Rule with strict hairstyle' or 'No rule (only hygiene)'. The option with the majority of votes becomes the new school rule. This respects human rights (freedom of expression) and democracy because the decision comes from the people (students) through voting, not from a single authority.

enonce.texte: Imagine you see this campaign poster before a municipal election in Douala. The candidate says: 'Vote for me! I will reduce taxes for all citizens. I will build a new hospital. I will listen to every tribe equally. Remember: a good representative respects diversity.'
images: A colorful political campaign poster in Cameroon. The poster has the Cameroonian flag colors (green, red, yellow). There is a photo of a smiling candidate in traditional attire. Text on the poster: 'Vote for CHANGE!', 'Better healthcare for all', 'Unity in diversity'. The candidate's name is 'Marie Ngoa'.

questions.texte: Analyze this poster using your knowledge of democracy. Answer the following questions in full sentences.

sousQuestions.texte: Identify two democratic values mentioned or shown in this poster. Explain where you see them.
sousQuestions.reponse: Two democratic values are: 1) Free elections ('Vote for me' implies citizens have the right to choose). 2) Equality/Respect for diversity ('listen to every tribe equally' and 'Unity in diversity').

sousQuestions.texte: What is the role of a 'representative' according to the poster? Do you think this is realistic? Justify your opinion.
sousQuestions.reponse: According to the poster, a representative should reduce taxes, build hospitals, and listen equally to all ethnic groups. This is partially realistic because a representative can propose laws and projects, but they cannot always keep every promise due to limited budget or national laws. However, listening equally to all citizens is a fundamental duty in a democracy.

sousQuestions.texte: If you were a citizen, what question would you ask this candidate before voting? Write one Wh-question and explain why it is important.
sousQuestions.reponse: Question: 'Where will you get the money to build the new hospital and reduce taxes at the same time?' Explanation: This question is important because citizens need to know if the candidate's promises are realistic and how public money will be managed (transparency).

intitule: Writing a Letter to Your Representative

enonce.texte:
Your neighborhood in Bamenda has a problem: the main water pipe is broken, and people have no clean water for two weeks. Write a formal letter to your elected representative in the local government. In your letter, you must:

  • Introduce yourself as a citizen.
  • Describe the problem clearly.
  • Use at least three words from Lesson 18 (democracy, government, election, vote, citizen, law, representative, human rights).
  • Request a specific action.

questions.reponse:
Dear Honorable Representative,

My name is Paul Mbarga, and I am a citizen of your constituency in Bamenda. I voted for you in the last election because you promised to improve our community.

I am writing to inform you about a serious problem. For two weeks, the main water pipe on Freedom Street has been broken. As a result, more than 200 families have no clean water. This situation affects our human rights, especially the right to health and clean water.

I respectfully request that your government take immediate action to repair the pipe or provide water tanks for the affected families. As our elected representative, you have the power to solve this problem.

Thank you for your attention to this urgent matter.

Sincerely,
Paul Mbarga
